suggestions.topic: EIP-7904 formal status change decision for Glamsterdam
suggestions.rationale: Deferred from ACDE #237 pending Geth benchmarks by end of May. All clients reportedly hit 100M gas/sec with BAL optimizations, suggesting EIP-7904 should be formally DFI'd.

suggestions.topic: glamsterdam-devnet-6 launch readiness and scope confirmation
suggestions.rationale: Target was ~2 weeks from June 4 (around June 18). Scope includes EIP-8246, EIP-7997, EIP-8038, EIP-2780 with preliminary repricing values. Need readiness update from client teams.

suggestions.topic: glamsterdam-devnet-5 stability and client onboarding status
suggestions.rationale: Launched June 4 with 2 ELs + 4 CLs; Teku onboarding post-fork. Need status on stability, additional client readiness, and any issues discovered.

suggestions.topic: Hegota headliner selection from PFI'd proposals
suggestions.rationale: Multiple EIPs PFI'd across recent calls: EIP-8188, EIP-8182, EIP-4758, EIP-8131, EIP-8279, EIP-7979, EIP-8173, EIP-7851, EIP-8151. Time to begin narrowing to headliner candidates.

Deferred from glamsterdam-devnet-4 pending BAL Discord discussion. Edge case affects when delegation address is added (only if gas/value/stack-depth checks pass). Decision needed for future devnet inclusion.

suggestions.topic: EIP-8037 simplification status and bal-devnet-7 results
suggestions.rationale: Simplification exploration requested in ACDE #235; bal-devnet-7 was final BAL-specific devnet with 8037 fixes. Update needed on complexity reduction efforts and any remaining concerns from testing.

suggestions.topic: History pruning target finalization (38-day reorg depth)
suggestions.rationale: ACDE #236 action item to update reorg-depth EIP around 38-day target and gather client feedback. Related to EIP-4444 one year, weak subjectivity 18 days, blob expiry 18 days from ACDE #234 discussion.

suggestions.topic: EIP-8254 deposit limit resolution and 200M gas floor path
suggestions.rationale: PFI'd in ACDE #236 as potential path to 200M gas floor, but deposit limit creates block building complexity. Ongoing discussion needed; not blocking unless 200M target is imminent.
